mode: MODE 1 (ISS-441 verdict patch) + new Phase 2 holistic findings (ISS-449 to ISS-452) + ISS-446 WIN-023 improvement
note: ISS-441 (WIN-054 verdict change) is being patched directly even though it appears in EXP-005 queue — the verdict change is unambiguous and does not require full taxonomy analysis. EXP-005 continues for ISS-442/443/444 (structural taxonomy questions). New issues ISS-449 to ISS-452 created from HOL-TONE review cross-reference checks.

patches.rationale: HOL-TAXONOMY: El Gordo is a genuine 6.2sigma LCDM anomaly. The dome is not misrepresenting data. 'Misleading' implies cherry-picking or deception; the correct verdict is 'Not Demonstrated' — dome cites a real anomaly but provides no cosmological structure formation model, no alternative collision velocity prediction, and no derivation from dome geometry. Curmudgeon correctly notes dome's argument is 'this anomaly exists therefore dome is possible', not 'dome predicts this anomaly.'

patches.find: Links geomagnetic event to theological timeline without mechanism
patches.replace: Geomagnetic excursion ~950 CE is well-documented in archaeomagnetic records (Campuzano 2019), but dome offers no mechanism linking Schumann frequency changes to pole position — the date is extracted from published literature, not derived from dome geometry
patches.rationale: HOL-TAXONOMY/ISS-446: Current finding dismisses the geomagnetic event without acknowledging it is real. A dome defender can correctly object 'You are denying a documented paleomagnetic event.' New finding acknowledges the kernel of truth (real excursion, real data) before explaining why it is not a dome WIN (no mechanism, no independent derivation).

patches.find: The ~950 AD date is taken from published paleomagnetic literature (Campuzano 2019), not derived from dome geometry. Linking it to a theological timeline without a physical mechanism is unfalsifiable.
patches.replace: The geomagnetic excursion near ~950 CE is well-supported by archaeomagnetic records (Campuzano et al. 2019) — the dome correctly identifies a real paleomagnetic event. However, the dome offers no mechanism connecting Schumann resonance frequency shifts to magnetic pole excursions, no derivation of the ~950 CE date from dome geometry, and no testable prediction distinguishing dome physics from standard geomagnetic models. The ~950 CE date is taken from published paleomagnetic literature, not derived from dome parameters.
patches.rationale: Updated verdict text to acknowledge kernel of truth before explaining the failure. Previous text jumped straight to 'unfalsifiable' without crediting the accurate observation. Retains the core critique (date extracted not predicted, no mechanism) while adding the fair acknowledgment that the event itself is real.

WIN-045 cross-references 'Section 4.5.4' (Solar Diameter) instead of 'Section 4.5.2' (Tidal Pattern). The tidal-pattern self-contradiction analysis is in Section 4.5.2. Section 4.5.4 is the solar angular diameter kill-shot — completely unrelated to tidal mechanics. WIN-046, WIN-050, and WIN-051 already correctly reference Section 4.5.2; WIN-045 and WIN-049 are the outliers.

WIN-049 has the same cross-reference error as WIN-045: references 'Section 4.5.4' (Solar Diameter) instead of 'Section 4.5.2' (Tidal Pattern). All five tidal WINs (045-051) should reference the Tidal Pattern section where the geometric argument is developed.

patches.find: the dome model smuggles in a globe parameter under a new name
patches.replace: the dome model incorporates a globe parameter under a new name
patches.rationale: HOL-TONE/ISS-451: 'smuggles in' imputes deliberate deceptive intent without evidence. The Evaluation Guide's Principle 2 commits us to distinguishing 'flawed from fraudulent.' The substantive critique (relabeling a globe-derived value as a dome prediction) is fully preserved with 'incorporates.' Removing the loaded verb strengthens credibility with fair-minded readers.

patches.find: The author avoids this by pretending the dome has uniform height
patches.replace: The author avoids this by treating the dome as having uniform height
patches.rationale: HOL-TONE/ISS-452: 'pretending' imputes deliberate deception. 'Treating the dome as having uniform height' accurately describes the modeling choice without asserting intent. The surrounding critique ('his V12 key discovery was specifically that it does not' have uniform height) fully preserves the substantive point that this assumption contradicts the model's own development history.
